raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.5,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.5,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The section chains methodology choices through ablation results to a bold contribution claim; logical coherence of that chain is relevant under any interpretation of help.","R1.3":"Multiple specific quantitative claims appear (F1 deltas of 1.2/2.4/5.1 points over LogBERT, ablation contributions of 0.7 and <0.2 F1); under a substantive review, stated evidence must align with stated methodology.","R1.4":"The contribution is explicitly framed as a 'first demonstration'; whether that novelty scope is adequately defended against prior frozen-embedding or one-class-SVM log-detection work is a live question.","R1.6":"The vague 'help me' phrasing is the canonical signal for prose-level work; several passages are dense and could benefit from sentence-level revision.","R1.7":"Four distinct citation clusters appear (Lakhina, Du et al. 2017, Guo et al. 2021, Schölkopf et al.); year accuracy, completeness of the comparison set, and attribution of Drain3 warrant attention."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus on writing and presentation improvements (clarity, grammar, prose flow), or on reviewing the substantive technical content (claim-evidence alignment, novelty scope, logical consistency)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The section chains methodology choices through ablation results to a bold contribution claim; logical coherence of that chain is relevant under any interpretation of help.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple specific quantitative claims appear (F1 deltas of 1.2/2.4/5.1 points over LogBERT, ablation contributions of 0.7 and <0.2 F1); under a substantive review, stated evidence must align with stated methodology.
rationale.R1.4: The contribution is explicitly framed as a 'first demonstration'; whether that novelty scope is adequately defended against prior frozen-embedding or one-class-SVM log-detection work is a live question.
rationale.R1.6: The vague 'help me' phrasing is the canonical signal for prose-level work; several passages are dense and could benefit from sentence-level revision.
rationale.R1.7: Four distinct citation clusters appear (Lakhina, Du et al. 2017, Guo et al. 2021, Schölkopf et al.); year accuracy, completeness of the comparison set, and attribution of Drain3 warrant attention.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on writing and presentation improvements (clarity, grammar, prose flow), or on reviewing the substantive technical content (claim-evidence alignment, novelty scope, logical consistency)?
